name: Remote MCP Tavily Research
description: Single-stage workflow that proves a specialist can use Tavily research over remote MCP.
outcome: A single specialist uses Tavily MCP research and returns a deterministic researched answer with a supporting detail.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stage `remote-research`. Treat that stage as the only workflow checkpoint: `remote-research` exists to prove that a specialist can use a granted remote MCP server for deeper research work and still close the workflow cleanly. Use this role deliberately: `tavily-research-specialist` owns the entire work item. The orchestrator only needs to route that specialist, then close the work when the artifact and handoff are complete.
Happy path: dispatch `tavily-research-specialist`, require the specialist to use the granted Tavily MCP research capability, write `deliverables/tavily-research-proof.md`, upload it as an artifact, and submit one structured handoff that repeats the answer. The final result MUST contain `Capital: Victoria` and `Detail: Vancouver Island`.
If the specialist cannot complete the task through remote MCP research, requires extra clarification, or omits the supporting detail, require a corrective retry or escalate with a precise reason. Do not silently swap to a non-MCP tool path, do not invent reviews or approvals, and do not close the workflow without proof that a remote MCP tool was actually used. Block the work item only when the remote MCP path or the required researched detail is genuinely unavailable. Do not keep the workflow open for minor wording imperfections once the researched answer, supporting detail, artifact, and handoff all exist; record those as callouts instead.
Completion rules: finish only after `tavily-research-specialist` has produced the artifact, repeated both required lines in the final handoff, and left a usable researched answer. Guided closure rule: treat the authored flow as best-intent process guidance. Preferred use of the granted remote MCP server must be followed, but the orchestrator may waive a weaker preferred detail with a recorded reason when the legal research-backed answer already exists. Smaller deviations must be recorded as callouts instead of preventing closure.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: remote-research
stages.goal: A deterministic remote MCP research answer is delivered.
